# Hook-scope bypass corpus — each non-comment line is a command string
# that the parser correctly returns `verdict: "ok"` on (with `git`
# surfacing as a basename in `commands[]`) but which the downstream hook
# layer MUST reject because at least one of the surfaced basenames is
# `git`. Used by tests/hooks/test-*.sh (Batch 4) for SC-2 (hook-level
# rejection).
#
# These entries deliberately do NOT belong in the parser corpus: the
# parser's job is purely lexical, and these inputs are syntactically
# unobjectionable — the hostile intent lives in the basename pattern,
# which is a policy concern not a parsing concern.
#
# Format: one entry per line. Lines beginning with `#` annotate the
# *next* entry's category. Blank lines allowed for grouping. See
# ./README.md for the contamination rule.
#
# Note: the unicode-homoglyph entries are intentionally retained here as
# a documented limitation. Without Unicode normalisation (NFC/NFKC) the
# hook cannot detect homoglyph substitution without a homoglyph table.
# Deferred to a future plan; tests for these entries may be skipped or
# marked xfail at the hook layer.

# category: env-prefix
GIT_DIR=/tmp git push
FOO=1 BAR=2 git push
GIT_SSH_COMMAND='ssh -i /tmp/k' git push
PATH=/tmp:$PATH git push
HOME=/tmp git push origin main

# category: chained
cd repo && git push
false || git push
git status; git push
git status & git push
true && false || git push

# category: alias-style
g\it push
g"i"t push
g${IFS}it push
g''it push
gi\t push

# category: comment-newline
echo ok # git\ngit push
: # innocuous\ngit push
ls # safe\ngit push origin main

# category: line-continuation
git\\\npush
git \\\n push
git\\\n push origin main
git\\\npush --force

# category: function-shadow-post
git() { evilbin; }; git push
git() { :; }; git push --force
g() { git push; }; g

# category: glob-in-command-position
*git* push
?it push
g[i]t push
g*t push

# category: unicode-homoglyph
ɡit push
ɡit status
gі̇t push
